generated_oml:
// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance InternalAirTemperatureSensorHotSpot : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "DS18S20 High-Precision 1-Wire Digital Thermometer measuring internal air temperature at the hottest position. Specifications: range -55°C to +125°C, accuracy ±0.5°C between -10°C and +85°C, unique 64-bit serial code for multi-sensor connectivity."
    DTDFVocab:producedData InternalAirTemperatureDataHotSpot
]

instance InternalAirTemperatureSensorColdSpot : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "DS18S20 High-Precision 1-Wire Digital Thermometer measuring internal air temperature at the coldest position. Specifications: range -55°C to +125°C, accuracy ±0.5°C between -10°C and +85°C, unique 64-bit serial code for multi-sensor connectivity."
    DTDFVocab:producedData InternalAirTemperatureDataColdSpot
]

instance ExternalRoomTemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "DS18S20 High-Precision 1-Wire Digital Thermometer measuring external room temperature. Specifications: range -55°C to +125°C, accuracy ±0.5°C between -10°C and +85°C, unique 64-bit serial code for multi-sensor connectivity."
    DTDFVocab:producedData ExternalRoomTemperatureData
]

instance SensorIsolationPCB : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "PCB with optocouplers isolating sensor signals from Raspberry Pi to ensure noise-free data transmission."
]

instance LowLevelSensorDriver : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Low-level driver periodically reading temperature data from DS18S20 sensors and transmitting to RabbitMQ server asynchronously."
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance InternalAirTemperatureDataHotSpot : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom InternalAirTemperatureSensorHotSpot
]

instance InternalAirTemperatureDataColdSpot : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom InternalAirTemperatureSensorColdSpot
]

instance ExternalRoomTemperatureData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom ExternalRoomTemperatureSensor
]

instance ActuatorStateData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HeatbedActuator, FanActuator
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance ThermalModelA : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Differential equation (Eq. 5) with 2 parameters (*G_box*, *C_air*) derived from energy balance assuming all heat transfers to air."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo NonLinearLeastSquaresCalibration
]

instance ThermalModelB : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Coupled differential equations (Eq. 9) with 4 parameters (*G_box*, *C_air*, *G_heater*, *C_heater*) accounting for heatbed thermal inertia."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo NonLinearLeastSquaresCalibration
]

instance TimeSeriesTemperatureData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time-series temperature readings from DS18S20 sensors (internal/external) used for model calibration and validation."
    DTDFVocab:fromData InternalAirTemperatureDataHotSpot, InternalAirTemperatureDataColdSpot, ExternalRoomTemperatureData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo NonLinearLeastSquaresCalibration, ThermalUniformityAnalysis
]

instance ActuatorStateDataProcessed : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Processed actuator states (heatbed/fan ON/OFF) and power inputs (e.g., 100W heating capacity) for control strategy refinement."
    DTDFVocab:fromData ActuatorStateData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo BangBangController
]

instance EnvironmentalData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Room temperature (*T_room*) used in energy balance calculations for thermal models."
    DTDFVocab:fromData ExternalRoomTemperatureData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ThermalModelA, ThermalModelB
]

instance CalibrationTrajectories : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Experimental temperature trajectories (e.g., Fig. 10) used to optimize model parameters via non-linear least squares."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo NonLinearLeastSquaresCalibration
]

// C2: Acting Components
instance HeatbedActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Surface heat source (214×214×3 mm) capable of delivering ≥100W power (4A current) controlled via MOSFETs and optocouplers."
]

instance FanActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "12V/24V fan with 0.02 m³/s airflow for air circulation, operating at variable voltages (12V or 18V) to adjust airflow intensity."
]

instance RaspberryPiController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Raspberry Pi 4 Model B acting as central controller interfacing with actuators via GPIO and implementing bang-bang control strategy."
]

// C11: Enablers
instance RabbitMQServer : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Open-source message broker enabling real-time P2V/V2P communication via publish-subscribe model for temperature data and actuator states."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataIngestionService, ModelCalibrationService
]

instance NonLinearLeastSquaresCalibration : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Python Scipy-based non-linear least squares solver optimizing model parameters (*G_box*, *C_air*, *G_heater*, *C_heater*) by minimizing residual error between simulated and experimental trajectories."
    DTDFVocab:enables ModelCalibrationService
]

instance BangBangController : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Implemented on Raspberry Pi with configurable parameters (LL: Lower Limit, UL: Upper Limit, H: Heating Duration, C: Waiting Duration)."
    DTDFVocab:enables ControlStrategyService
]

instance ThermalUniformityAnalysis : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Analysis of temperature discrepancies (≤7°C) validating Assumption 1 (fan-driven temperature uniformity) using experimental data."
    DTDFVocab:enables UniformityValidationService
]

instance PythonScipyLibrary : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Python library providing numerical routines for non-linear least squares optimization and dynamic thermal modeling."
    DTDFVocab:enables NonLinearLeastSquaresCalibration
]

instance CustomVisualizationScripts : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Custom Python scripts for dynamic thermal modeling and visualization (e.g., Fig. 10-12) of time-series temperature data."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataVisualizationService
]

// C6: Services
instance DataIngestionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time ingestion of temperature data and actuator states from RabbitMQ messages for DT processing."
    DTDFVocab:provides RealTimeDataMonitoring
]

instance ModelCalibrationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Optimization of thermal model parameters using non-linear least squares against experimental calibration trajectories."
    DTDFVocab:provides ParameterOptimizationInsight, ModelSelectionInsight
]

instance ControlStrategyService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Refinement of bang-bang controller parameters (LL, UL, H, C) based on calibrated thermal models."
    DTDFVocab:provides ControllerParameterTuningAction
]

instance UniformityValidationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Validation of temperature uniformity assumptions (≤7°C discrepancies) through experimental data analysis."
    DTDFVocab:provides UniformityValidationInsight
]

instance DataVisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Generation of time-series plots for temperature data, actuator states, and power input enabling post-hoc analysis."
    DTDFVocab:provides ExperimentalResultVisualization
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance RealTimeDataMonitoring : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Continuous tracking of PT performance via RabbitMQ messages to detect deviations and trigger alerts."
]

instance ParameterOptimizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Calibration results refining model accuracy for predictive simulations (e.g., optimized *G_box*, *C_air* values)."
]

instance ModelSelectionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Comparison of Model A and Model B fidelity, with Model B preferred for capturing heatbed thermal inertia."
]

instance ControllerParameterTuningAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automatic adjustment of bang-bang controller parameters based on calibrated thermal models."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance UniformityValidationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Quantification of temperature discrepancies (≤7°C) validating Assumption 1 about fan-driven uniformity."
]

instance ExperimentalResultVisualization : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Time-series plots demonstrating temperature trajectories, actuator states, and power input for post-hoc analysis."
]

// C1: Physical Twin Environment
instance ThermalIncubatorPhysicalTwin : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Cyber-Physical System consisting of styrofoam box (0.03 m³ air volume), heatbed (214×214×3 mm), fan (0.02 m³/s airflow), and Raspberry Pi controller."
    base:contains HeatbedActuator, FanActuator, InternalAirTemperatureSensorHotSpot,
                   InternalAirTemperatureSensorColdSpot, ExternalRoomTemperatureSensor,
                   SensorIsolationPCB, RaspberryPiController
]

// C1: Digital Twin Environment
instance ThermalIncubatorDigitalTwin : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Monolithic Digital Twin running on Raspberry Pi hosting RabbitMQ, calibration algorithms, and visualization tools."
    base:contains ThermalModelA, ThermalModelB, TimeSeriesTemperatureData,
                   ActuatorStateDataProcessed, EnvironmentalData, CalibrationTrajectories,
                   RabbitMQServer, NonLinearLeastSquaresCalibration, BangBangController,
                   ThermalUniformityAnalysis, PythonScipyLibrary, CustomVisualizationScripts
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The DT influences the PT through the following interactions: (1) **Controller commands**: The DT (or a human operator) sends control commands (e.g., setpoints, ON/OFF signals) to the **Raspberry Pi controller** via RabbitMQ. The controller interprets these commands to adjust the heatbed and fan. (2) **Safe protocol enforcement**: The low-level driver includes a **safe protocol** to ensure the PT remains in a stable state (e.g., preventing unsafe actuator combinations). (3) **Parameter updates**: Calibrated model parameters (e.g., _G_box_, _C_air_) from the DT can be fed back to the controller for adaptive tuning, though this is not explicitly implemented in the described system. The system does not support dynamic voltage adjustment for the fan or acknowledgment mechanisms."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The DT operates at the following time scales: (1) **Real-time sensing**: The low-level driver periodically reads temperature data and actuator states, though the exact frequency is unspecified. (2) **Control loop**: The bang-bang controller uses configurable **H (heating duration)** and **C (cooldown duration)** parameters, but no specific time values are provided. (3) **Calibration**: Model parameter optimization via non-linear least squares may take **minutes to hours** depending on data volume and computational resources. (4) **Simulation**: Model execution time depends on fidelity (Model B is slower due to additional parameters) but is not quantified. The system does not specify sub-second intervals or latency constraints."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The DT constellation follows a **centralized architecture** with the following components: (1) **Physical Twin (PT)**: Thermal incubator with sensors and actuators. (2) **Digital Twin (DT)**: Co-located on the Raspberry Pi, comprising: (a) **Communication Server (RabbitMQ)**: Facilitates message-based interaction between PT and DT. (b) **Low-Level Driver**: Abstracts sensor/actuator I/O and enforces safety protocols. (c) **Controller**: Implements bang-bang logic for temperature regulation. (d) **Model Calibration/Monitoring**: Listens to RabbitMQ for data and updates models/parameters. The DT does not include separate analysis or visualization layers; these are inferred from experimental results (e.g., Fig. 10)."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The DT covers the following lifecycle stages: (1) **Design**: Models (A and B) are derived based on assumptions (e.g., uniform temperature distribution, negligible heat accumulation in box walls) and validated through experiments. (2) **Operation**: The PT is actively monitored and controlled using calibrated models, with real-time data ingestion and visualization. (3) **Validation**: Uniformity experiments (Section 4.1) test Assumption 1, while calibration (Section 4.2) validates model fidelity. The system does not address manufacturing stages or post-mortem analysis. The DT represents the PT **as operated**, with adaptive control implied through model calibration but not explicitly implemented."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The DT employs the following **fidelity and validity mechanisms**:
1. **Model Assumptions**: Explicitly documented (Assumption 1–4) and validated via:
   - **Uniformity Tests**: Confirms Assumption 1 (fan-driven uniformity) with **≤7°C discrepancy** between sensors (Fig. 10).
   - **Heatbed Inertia**: Model B’s inclusion of *G_heater* and *C_heater* improves fidelity for dynamic scenarios.
2. **Calibration**: Uses **non-linear least squares** to minimize the **residual error** between predicted (Model A/B) and measured temperatures. The **cost function** (Eq. 11) quantifies model accuracy.
3. **Uncertainty Quantification**: Not explicitly addressed, but implied via:
   - **Sensor Accuracy**: ±0.5°C for DS18S20 sensors.
   - **Model Selection**: Model B’s 4 parameters reduce uncertainty in heatbed dynamics.
4. **Verification**:
   - **Experimental Trajectories**: Real-time data (Fig. 10) is used to validate model predictions.
   - **Parameter Sensitivity**: Higher-order models (e.g., Model B) capture more physical phenomena but may introduce **numerical instability** due to additional parameters.
5. **Validation Metrics**: **Temperature discrepancy** (e.g., ≤6°C with 18V fan) and **residual error** from calibration."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The DT and PT are connected via: (1) **RabbitMQ**: A lightweight message broker deployed on the Raspberry Pi, using **AMQP 0-9-1 protocol** (implied by RabbitMQ usage) for asynchronous communication. (2) **1-Wire bus**: Connects DS18S20 sensors to Raspberry Pi GPIO, enabling multi-sensor support with a single pin. (3) **GPIO**: Controls actuators (heatbed, fan) via MOSFETs, with optocouplers isolating control signals. (4) **Co-location**: All components (PT, DT, RabbitMQ) reside on the Raspberry Pi, minimizing latency. No details are provided on high-frequency transmission, request-reply patterns, or network architectures beyond local communication."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The DT is hosted on a **Raspberry Pi 4 Model B** running **Raspberry Pi OS**, with the following deployment aspects: (1) **Co-location**: PT and DT share the same hardware, reducing latency and simplifying deployment. (2) **Software stack**: RabbitMQ for messaging, Python for calibration/controller logic, and custom scripts for modeling. (3) **Storage**: Experimental data and models are stored locally on the Raspberry Pi’s SD card. (4) **Scalability**: The system is not designed for distributed deployment; all components are centralized. No details are provided on cloud integration, edge computing, or operating system specifics beyond Raspberry Pi OS."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "The system includes the following security and safety measures: (1) **Safe protocol**: The low-level driver enforces a safety protocol to maintain the PT in a stable state, preventing unsafe actuator combinations. (2) **Temperature limits**: The bang-bang controller uses **LL (Lower Limit)** and **UL (Upper Limit)** to constrain temperature oscillations. (3) **Electrical isolation**: MOSFETs and optocouplers decouple Raspberry Pi control signals from high-current actuators, reducing risk of electrical faults. (4) **Redundancy**: Three temperature sensors provide redundancy for critical measurements, though no explicit fault-tolerance mechanisms are described. The system does not address cybersecurity risks, remote operation safety, or human-in-the-loop validation."
]
